question_text: Define the spring constant and explain what it tells us about a spring.
correct_answer: The spring constant (k) is a measure of how stiff or rigid a spring is. It is the force required to stretch (or compress) the spring by one unit of length. A larger spring constant means a stiffer spring that requires more force to stretch.
explanation: The spring constant is a key property that quantifies a spring's resistance to deformation and determines how much it will extend under a given force.
